mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.5%, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stationary federal funds rate creates a negative real rate environment that compresses equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL increases by more than 0.5% monthly while FEDFUNDS remains unchanged, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly declines in industrial production signal immediate supply chain bottlenecks that force equity market repricing, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines of greater than 1% fail to precede negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 60% of observed cases, or the mechanism reverses direction during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real GDP growth reflect expanding aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 60-day forward returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01 across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts surpass 1.2 million units, construction sector momentum and consumer confidence surge, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns turn negative in over 50% of months where HOUST crosses the 1200 threshold, or the signal loses significance during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening that boosts consumer spending expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive returns when PAYEMS rises by more than 200k while UNRATE falls, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly spikes in retail sales indicate immediate consumer demand strength that lifts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: RSAFS increases fail to precede positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or the predictive relationship vanishes during mid-cycle expansion regimes.
